other_observers: I alerted Ric Olson and Brandon Percival and they came and found the bird independently; Bill Maynard
specific_location: Southeast side of Rose Pond at Chico Basin Ranch on the earthen dam.
description: Easily recognized as a towhee. Plumage on head and dorsally a rich warm brown. Flanks brightest part of bird, a rich warm rufous. Wings same coloration as nape and back, a warm brown. Noticeable white "flag" at base of primaries, contrasting strongly with wing coloration. Bill sparrow-like and dark gray, tail dark brown, throat same color as face forming a bib. Head, back and wings warm brown. Breast and belly white. Leg color appeared brownish.Behaviors: This bird was in the tumbleweeds along the earthen dam at Rose Pond at Chico Basin Ranch. It eventually worked its way up into near the top of a small (20') tall Siberian elm. Bird called frequently. First seen on the ground. Not real shy although liked to stay partially hidden.Call: call given was a loud rising "shhhwwink" quite different from Spotted Towhee.Plumage: adult
